tools.name: ImageDescription
tools.description: A useful tool that returns a brief description of the input image.

tools.name: OCR
tools.description: This tool can recognize all text on the input image.

outputs.description: OCR results, include bbox in x1, y1, x2, y2 format and the recognized text.

tools.name: RegionAttributeDescription
tools.description: Describe the attribute of a region of the input image.

inputs.name: bbox
inputs.description: The bbox coordinate in the format of `(x1, y1, x2, y2)`

dialogs.content: What is the purpose of the MRI imaging in this context? Please use the provided figure to support the answer.

dialogs.thought: Orient to figure type and whether it defines how MRI is being evaluated.

content.content: The image is a text-only figure listing a 1–6 scoring system describing how breast MRI results affected clinical management, ranging from confusing/incorrect findings to measurable changes in the original treatment plan.

dialogs.thought: Extract the exact wording of the scoring criteria to infer the stated purpose of MRI in this context.

content.content:
(10, 6, 820, 66) 1 = MR results were confusing or incorrect and stimulated more
(10, 62, 820, 106) studies to clarify the situation
(10, 120, 820, 164) 2 = MR results were confusing or incorrect but did not stimulate
(10, 162, 320, 206) more studies
(10, 222, 820, 266) 3 = MR results had no impact on original therapy plan
(10, 282, 820, 326) 4= MR result did not cause a change in the original plan but
(10, 322, 820, 366) increased the confidence in that plan
(10, 382, 820, 426) 5 = MRI results could have caused a measurable change in the
(10, 422, 260, 466) original plan
(10, 482, 820, 526) 6 = MR results did cause a measurable change in the original
(10, 522, 120, 566) plan.

arguments.attribute: Summarize what the scoring system is assessing about MRI results

dialogs.thought: Confirm the figure explicitly frames MRI as added value beyond conventional imaging by focusing on impact on management.

content.content: This region lists score definitions indicating the degree to which MRI findings add information and influence care: from confusing/incorrect findings (scores 1–2), to no impact (3), increased confidence without changing the plan (4), possible measurable change (5), and definite measurable change in the original plan (6).

dialogs.content: In this context, the purpose of MRI imaging is to evaluate its added value in comparison to conventional breast imaging techniques. The team assigned a score from 1 to 6, considering the additional contribution made by the MRI study beyond what was available with conventional breast imaging. This assessment helps to determine the usefulness and effectiveness of MRI imaging in providing more detailed or accurate information for diagnosis, treatment planning, or monitoring of breast-related conditions.

whitelist: In this context, the purpose of MRI imaging is to evaluate its added value in comparison to conventional breast imaging techniques. The team assigned a score from 1 to 6, considering the additional contribution made by the MRI study beyond what was available with conventional breast imaging. This assessment helps to determine the usefulness and effectiveness of MRI imaging in providing more detailed or accurate information for diagnosis, treatment planning, or monitoring of breast-related conditions.
